description:
You are a database reliability engineer.  
    A helper script that performs the actual dump already exists at  
    /home/user/backup.sh  (feel free to inspect it, but you do **not** have to modify it).

    Your goal is to author **both** a user-level systemd timer *and* a cron specification that
    will run /home/user/backup.sh every day at **03:15** in the morning.

    1. Systemd artefacts  
       a. Service file: /home/user/.config/systemd/user/db-backup.service  
       b. Timer  file:  /home/user/.config/systemd/user/db-backup.timer  

       Exact required contents (including blank lines, capitalisation and the very last
       trailing newline!) are:

       ----- db-backup.service -----
       [Unit]
       Description=Database backup

       [Service]
       Type=oneshot
       ExecStart=/home/user/backup.sh
       (newline at end)
       -----------------------------

       ----- db-backup.timer -----
       [Unit]
       Description=Daily database backup timer

       [Timer]
       OnCalendar=*-*-* 03:15:00
       Persistent=true

       [Install]
       WantedBy=default.target
       (newline at end)
       -----------------------------

       • The two files must reside exactly in the directory shown above  
       • Make sure the directory exists beforehand (create it if missing).  
       • After the files are in place, no further enable/start action is required for
         the purpose of this exercise.

    2. Cron artefact  
       Create a text file named /home/user/backup_cron_entry.txt that contains exactly  
       the single line below (again: include the final newline):

       15 3 * * * /home/user/backup.sh
       (newline at end)

    3. File modes  
       /home/user/backup.sh must be executable (chmod +x) when you finish.

    4. Verification log (required)  
       When you have completed everything, append this single line to the file
       /home/user/backup_task.log (create the file if it does not exist):

       TASK FINISHED

       The log must therefore end with the text “TASK FINISHED\n”.

    Summary of what the automated checker will evaluate:
       • Presence and **exact** contents (byte-for-byte) of the two systemd unit files.  
       • Presence and **exact** contents of /home/user/backup_cron_entry.txt.  
       • backup.sh is executable.  
       • /home/user/backup_task.log exists and its **last** line reads exactly
         “TASK FINISHED”.

    All actions can be done from a normal, non-privileged user shell.  
    No root access is available or necessary.  
    Good luck!
truth:
<!--
      Pre-existing resource:
        /home/user/backup.sh   (already present before task starts)
        Contents:
          #!/bin/bash
          echo "backup executed"
          (newline at end)

      Final state the grader will check:

      1. /home/user/.config/systemd/user/db-backup.service
         Byte-exact content:
             [Unit]
             Description=Database backup

             [Service]
             Type=oneshot
             ExecStart=/home/user/backup.sh
             (newline)

      2. /home/user/.config/systemd/user/db-backup.timer
         Byte-exact content:
             [Unit]
             Description=Daily database backup timer

             [Timer]
             OnCalendar=*-*-* 03:15:00
             Persistent=true

             [Install]
             WantedBy=default.target
             (newline)

      3. /home/user/backup_cron_entry.txt
         Byte-exact content:
             15 3 * * * /home/user/backup.sh
             (newline)

      4. /home/user/backup.sh
         Must have executable bit (ugo+x).  Content is unchanged.

      5. /home/user/backup_task.log
         Must exist; last line is exactly:
             TASK FINISHED
             (newline)

      No requirement for systemctl enabling/starting will be tested.
    -->
name: task_000000_f1921968
